_schema: Site-level content: org identity, hero, stat band (each stat carries a source reference — no unsourced numbers), Questions Worth Asking rail (Critical-Inquiry content given a standing home at parity with news), quick actions per audience.

org.name: The Generator
org.full_name: The Generator at the C. Dean Metropoulos Institute for Technology and Entrepreneurship

hero.lede_prefix: We are an interdisciplinary AI lab reimagining the future of
hero.mission: We empower innovators to explore, teach, and research the transformative potential of AI in education, business, and society.
hero.what_is_this: The Generator is Babson College's interdisciplinary artificial intelligence lab, part of the C. Dean Metropoulos Institute for Technology and Entrepreneurship. Faculty, students, and partners explore what AI makes possible — and what it puts at risk — in education, business, and society. Everyone is welcome here: enthusiasts, skeptics, and the simply curious, no AI experience required.

of Babson faculty peer-trained in AI concepts and tools
institutions convened by the Writing and AI Symposium
small business owners in AI training — a goal in progress

questions_worth_asking.intro: An AI lab that only cheerleads isn't doing its job. These questions — from our own faculty and events — stay open on purpose.

items.quote: How do you use AI without having it use us?
items.attribution: Mindful AI Playground — AI/ML Empowerment & AI for Research & Writing Labs

items.quote: Maybe instead of problem-solving, we should teach students to define problems, configure problems, and create problems. Be a problem creator, rather than a problem solver.
items.attribution: Xinghua Li, Co-leader, AI Arts, Media & Performance Lab

items.quote: How might AI either intensify totalitarianism or propel resistance movements in an age of surveillance, empire, and cyber-warfare?
items.attribution: AI Ethics & Society Lab — focus question

items.quote: What human skills and competences will new forms of work require?

quick_actions._note: Audience keys drive the homepage audience-selector reordering. Each card: concrete value + status + single CTA (student-panel requirement).

cards.title: New to AI? Start here
cards.value: A 10-minute on-ramp: what The Generator is, one video, and the one event this season built for beginners.

cards.title: AI Credits — free compute
cards.value: $1,000 grants from Babson (fast turnaround) or $1,000–$25,000 via Microsoft for Startups for your venture.

cards.title: Lab internships
cards.value: Research, design, and communications roles inside the labs — multilingual AI, future-of-work research, Republic 2.0.

cards.title: AI Innovators Bootcamp
cards.value: A 1-day, no-prerequisites AI deep dive for small business owners, taught by Babson faculty with students at your side.

cards.title: B.R.A.I.N. — the Babson Regional AI Network
cards.value: Meetups across New England connecting business owners, technologists, and researchers around entrepreneurial AI.

cards.title: Stay in the loop
cards.value: Subscribe once: the faculty events calendar feed, or the digest email. The site comes to you.

cards.title: CollaboratorAI
cards.value: Find Babson faculty with overlapping research interests — an AI-assisted collaboration finder.

cards.title: Support The Generator
cards.value: Gifts fund student AI credits, the Buildathons, and the labs' public programming.
